title: A Superhydrophobic CNTs–MSiO2@MnO2 Cathode for Moisture-Tolerant Nonaqueous Li–O2 Batteries
authors: Tianyu Zhang, Guocan Hu, Hong Sun, Jie Li, Xiaochen Zhang, Qiang Li, Zhichao Xue, Mingfu Yu and Longfei Xu
abstract: Nonaqueous Li–O2 batteries offer high theoretical <mark>energy density</mark>, but their semi-open configuration makes them vulnerable to moisture ingress and water-driven degradation. Here we report an integrated CNTs-MSiO2@MnO2 superhydrophobic cathode for nonaqueous Li–O2 batteries. Rather than using an external waterproof membrane or surface coating, this design distributes methylated SiO2 nanoparticles within a conductive CNT/MnO2 framework, coupling water repellency, electronic conduction, catalytic activity and gas transport. The cathode shows a static water contact angle of 153.9° and a rolling ang…

title: Impact of Electrolyte Feed and Catalyst-Coated Substrate Properties on Cell Performance and Hydrogen Crossover in AEM Water Electrolysis
authors: Jan Witte, Patrick Trinke, Boris Bensmann, Maik Becker, Vinzent Olszok, Alfred P. Weber, Richard Hanke-Rauschenbach and Thomas Turek
abstract: Hydrogen crossover is a major challenge in anion exchange membrane water electrolysis (AEMWE), affecting both efficiency and operational <mark>safety</mark>. In this study, the combined effect of cathode feed configuration (wet vs dry cathode) and catalyst-coated substrate (CCS) properties on cell performance and hydrogen crossover are systematically investigated. Two cathode CCS types are evaluated, including a commercially available and an in-house manufactured variant prepared by hand-spraying (0.5 mg Pt), using identical anodic CCS. The in-house CCS delivers improved cell performance in wet cathode con…
